# Single-User Chat

> A reference SPA for `kaos-agents` showing transparent tool policy,
> citation surfaces, and the AgenticLoop's auto-elevation behavior.
> Single-tenant by design — bearer-token auth, no public routes.

This is the example app shipped with the
[kaos-ui](https://github.com/273v/kaos-ui) scaffolder
(`kaos-ui new web:spa`). It demonstrates the full backend +
frontend pattern for a `kaos-agents`-powered chat surface:
SSE streaming, dynamic tool policy, document upload, citation
extraction, and the four AgenticLoop events
(`tool_policy_elevated`, `capability_requested`, `goal_checked`,
`loop_terminated`).

## Project

- [README](https://github.com/273v/kaos-ui/blob/main/README.md):
  what kaos-ui is and how to scaffold a project from it.
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/273v/kaos-ui/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md):
  release notes including the AgenticLoop wire-up.
- [Example source](https://github.com/273v/kaos-ui/tree/main/examples/single-user-chat):
  the backend + SPA this app is built from.

## Architecture

- Backend: FastAPI on `kaos-agents` 0.1.0a4 (AgenticLoop) + `kaos-core`
  VFS, with httpx-proxied streaming to the kaos-agents `/v1/sessions/*`
  API. Bearer auth, single-tenant.
- Frontend: React 19 + Vite + TanStack Router + Tailwind 4, consuming
  the [`@273v/kaos-ui-react`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@273v/kaos-ui-react)
  component library.
- Streaming: SSE wire shape with 20 typed event variants (15 canonical
  kaos-agents + 5 kaos-ui extensions).

## Notes for crawlers

This is an *auth-gated single-user app*, not a public service. There is
no public content to index. The codebase is the artifact; visit the
GitHub repo above for sources and documentation.
